tools.name: generate_uml
tools.description: Generate any UML or diagram by type (class, sequence, mermaid, d2, etc.)

diagram_type.description: Type of diagram (e.g. class, sequence, activity, mermaid, d2)

code.description: Diagram code in the format for the chosen type

output_dir.description: Directory to save the generated image

output_format.description: Output format: svg, png, or pdf

theme.description: PlantUML theme (only for PlantUML diagram types)

resources.name: uml://types
resources.description: Returns available diagram types with backend, description, and supported formats (e.g. class, sequence, mermaid, d2). Use before generating to verify diagram_type.

resources.name: uml://templates
resources.description: Returns starter templates (PlantUML, Mermaid, D2, etc.) for each diagram type. Use to get minimal valid code to customize.

resources.name: uml://examples
resources.description: Returns example diagrams for each type (class, sequence, activity, etc.). Use as reference for syntax and structure.

resources.name: uml://formats
resources.description: Returns supported output formats (svg, png, pdf) per diagram type. Use to choose valid output_format for generate_uml.

resources.name: uml://server-info
resources.description: Returns server metadata: name, version, tools, prompts, Kroki URL. Use for discovery and health checks.

resources.name: uml://mermaid-examples
resources.description: Returns named Mermaid examples (sequence_api, gantt) for API-call flows and Gantt charts. Reference for mermaid_sequence_api and mermaid_gantt prompts.

resources.name: uml://bpmn-guide
resources.description: Structured reference for BPMN 2.0.2: start/end events, tasks, gateways, sequence flow, lanes, pools. Use with generate_uml (diagram_type 'bpmn') or bpmn_process_guide prompt.

resources.name: uml://workflow
resources.description: Recommended workflow: plan first (diagram type, elements, relationships), then call generate_uml with the final code. Use uml_diagram or uml_diagram_with_thinking prompt.

prompts.name: uml_diagram
prompts.description: Base prompt for UML diagram generation. Guides the model to produce diagram code (PlantUML, Mermaid, D2) for any diagram type including class, sequence, activity, use case, and more.

prompts.name: uml_diagram_with_thinking
prompts.description: Generate UML diagram with an explicit plan-then-generate workflow. Plan diagram type, elements, and relationships first, then output code and call generate_uml.

prompts.name: class_diagram
prompts.description: Generate UML class diagram from a natural language description. Produces PlantUML code with classes, attributes, methods, visibility, inheritance, composition, and associations.

prompts.name: sequence_diagram
prompts.description: Generate UML sequence diagram from a description. Produces PlantUML code with participants, lifelines, messages, activations, and optional return messages.

prompts.name: activity_diagram
prompts.description: Generate UML activity diagram from a description. Produces PlantUML code with start/end, activities, decisions, forks, joins, and swimlanes.

prompts.name: usecase_diagram
prompts.description: Generate UML use case diagram from a description. Produces PlantUML code with actors, use cases, system boundary, include/extend relationships, and associations.

prompts.name: mermaid_sequence_api
prompts.description: Produce a Mermaid sequence diagram for an API call flow: client, API, optional Auth/DB, request/response, and optional alt blocks for success vs error.

prompts.name: mermaid_gantt
prompts.description: Generate a Mermaid Gantt chart with title, dateFormat, sections, and tasks including dependencies (after) and durations.

prompts.name: bpmn_process_guide
prompts.description: Explain how to draw a BPMN process model. Covers start/end events, tasks, gateways (XOR, AND, OR), sequence flow, lanes, pools, aligned with BPMN 2.0.2.

prompts.name: convert_class_to_mermaid
prompts.description: Convert a class diagram (PlantUML code or prose description) into Mermaid classDiagram syntax, mapping visibility, relationships, and inheritance.
